---
title: Create a Next.js App
---

# Create a Next.js App

This runbook scaffolds a new [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) application in a GitHub repository and opens a pull request with the initial code.

## Pre-flight Checks

<Check
  id="check-bun"
  command="bun --version"
  title="Check if Bun is installed"
  description="Bun is required to scaffold the Next.js app. Install it from [bun.sh](https://bun.sh)."
  successMessage="Bun is installed!"
  failMessage="Bun is not installed. Please install it from https://bun.sh and try again."
/>

## Authenticate to GitHub

<GitHubAuth
  id="gh-auth"
  title="Connect to GitHub"
  description="Authenticate to create repositories and open pull requests"
/>

## Create a Repository

If you already have a repository, skip this step — we'll clone it in the next step either way.

<Command
  id="create-repo"
  command="gh repo create {{ .inputs.GithubOrgName }}/{{ .inputs.RepoName }} --public"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Create a GitHub repository"
  description="Creates a new public GitHub repository. If it already exists, skip this step and clone it below."
  successMessage="Repository created!"
  failMessage="Failed to create the repository. If it already exists, that's fine — proceed to the next step."
>
  <Inputs id="repo-inputs">
  ```yaml
  variables:
    - name: GithubOrgName
      type: string
      description: GitHub organization or username
      validations:
        - required
    - name: RepoName
      type: string
      description: Repository name
      default: my-next-app
      validations:
        - required
  ```
  </Inputs>
</Command>

## Clone the Repository

Clone the repository you created above, or an existing repository you'd like to add a Next.js app to.

<GitClone
  id="clone-repo"
  gitHubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Clone the Repository"
  description="Clone the repository so we can scaffold the Next.js app into it"
  prefilledUrl="https://github.com/gruntwork-test/empty-repo"
/>

## Scaffold the Next.js App

Choose your project options, then run `create-next-app` to scaffold the app into the cloned repository.

<Command
  id="create-next-app"
  path="scripts/create-next-app.sh"
  title="Run create-next-app"
  description="Scaffolds a new Next.js project with the selected options."
  successMessage="Next.js app scaffolded!"
  failMessage="Failed to scaffold the Next.js app."
>
  <Inputs id="next-app-options">
  ```yaml
  variables:
    - name: Language
      type: enum
      description: Programming language
      options: [TypeScript, JavaScript]
      default: TypeScript
    - name: UseTailwind
      type: enum
      description: Include Tailwind CSS for styling
      options: ["Yes", "No"]
      default: "Yes"
    - name: Bundler
      type: enum
      description: Development bundler
      options: [Turbopack, Webpack]
      default: Turbopack
    - name: ImportAlias
      type: string
      description: Import alias for the project
      default: "@/*"
  ```
  </Inputs>
</Command>

## Add a Custom Component

Let's add a sample `WelcomeCard` component to demonstrate adding custom files to the scaffolded app. Customize the default title and description, then generate it into the repo.

<Template id="welcome-card" path="templates/welcome-card" target="worktree" />

## Create a Pull Request

Now let's push everything to a new branch and open a pull request.

<GitHubPullRequest
  id="create-pr"
  githubAuthId="gh-auth"
  title="Open a Pull Request"
  description="Create a pull request with the scaffolded Next.js app and custom components"
  prefilledPullRequestTitle="feat: scaffold Next.js app"
  prefilledPullRequestDescription={"## What\n\nScaffolded a new Next.js application with:\n\n- TypeScript\n- Tailwind CSS\n- ESLint\n- App Router\n- Custom `WelcomeCard` component\n\n---\n\n*Generated by a [Runbook](https://github.com/gruntwork-io/runbooks).*"}
/>
